items.correct: Volume of air inhaled or exhaled in one normal breath at rest.
items.misconception: The maximum air your lungs can hold.
items.random_wrong: Air left in the lungs after maximum exhalation.
items.why: Students confuse tidal volume (resting breath, ~0.5L) with vital capacity (max breath, ~4-5L). Different lung volumes serve different purposes - tidal is the normal small ebb and flow.

items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.

items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).
Correct answer: Front of thigh. “Back of leg” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Bottom. “Foot” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many muscles in the quadriceps group?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
